data.title: Dan Ives Pounds the Table on These 3 Cyber Security Stocks
data.description:
Cyber security stocks have struggled in 2026 due to the AI-driven sell-off in software. However, five-star Wedbush analyst Dan Ives believes that this weakness could present a buying opportunity. In a recent note, he argued that artificial intelligence will ultimately act as a powerful tailwind for the sector. This is because as AI adoption expands across businesses, so does the need to protect data, applications, and endpoints.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, that view is based on the simple reality that hackers are also using AI, which is forcing companies to spend more on defense. According to Wedbush, some security vendors are actually raising their sales targets by as much as 30% this year due to strong demand. Even though IT budgets may face pressure, security spending is increasingly seen as non-negotiable. Still, bears argue that rising AI costs could crowd out other IT spending, thereby limiting overall growth. Nevertheless, Ives highlighted three companies that he believes are best positioned to benefit:

  • CrowdStrike (CRWD)
  • Palo Alto Networks (PANW)
  • Zscaler (ZS)

Notably, he called CrowdStrike the industry's "gold standard" due to the strength of its Falcon platform, and maintained a Buy rating with a $600 price target. Secondly, Ives said that Palo Alto Networks remains a top pick, thanks to its platform consolidation strategy and its CyberArk acquisition, which earned the stock a $225 target. Finally, Zscaler received a $350 price target from Ives as a result of its durable subscription growth tied to its Zero Trust model.

Which Cyber Security Stock Is the Better Buy?

Overall, out of the three stocks mentioned above, analysts think that ZS stock has the most room to run. In fact, Zscaler's average price target of $315.24 per share implies 82.7% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: MSTR Stock Dips: Mizuho Trims Target by 20% on 'BTC Weakness'
data.description:
Mizuho Securities analyst Dan Dolev lowered his price target on Strategy (MSTR) stock due to falling Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices, which drive much of the company's valuation via its massive BTC holdings. MSTR stock fell 2.5% in pre-market trading on the news. Nonetheless, Dolev remains constructive on the shares and maintained his Buy rating because of Strategy's $2.25 billion to $2.3 billion in reserves intended to cover 2–3 years of dividends.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Dolev compared the ongoing "crypto winter" to NYC's unprecedented brutal cold snaps. This freeze, he says, has driven Strategy's Bitcoin holding value (672,500 BTC) below its roughly $76,000 average cost basis.

Dolev Updates MSTR Model Post-Q4 Results

Following Strategy's Q4FY25 results, Dolev revised his end-2027 BTC price forecast to $128,000, down from $159,000. He highlighted Strategy's massive Q4 loss that was fueled almost entirely from unrealized markdowns on its Bitcoin holdings under the new fair-value accounting standards.

Strategy reported a staggering $12.4 billion net loss ($42.93 per share) in Q4, up from $670.8 million the prior year, alongside a $17.4 billion operating loss. The loss stemmed directly from Bitcoin's sharp drop into early 2026. Management stressed these are purely accounting losses under fair-value rules, not cash flow issues.

Why Dolev Stays Bullish on MSTR

Dolev cited three prime reasons for remaining bullish on Strategy stock:

  • MSTR remains the world's largest corporate holder of Bitcoin, with 22.8% BTC yield, within its target range.
  • MSTR has sufficient cash reserves of $2.25bn–$2.3bn to survive the crypto winter.
  • Historical BTC winters prove temporary, supporting long-term upside.

What Is the Prediction for MSTR Shares?

Despite the ongoing Bitcoin weakness, analysts remain optimistic about Strategy's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, MSTR has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 11 unanimous Buys. The average Strategy price target of $359.82 implies 168.8% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, MSTR shares have dropped 59.9%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Fall as AI Disruption Fears Continue to Drag Down Tech Stocks
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Tuesday morning, kicking off a holiday-shortened trading week in the red amid continued fears of artificial intelligence (AI) disrupting software and other businesses. Meanwhile, traders await key employment figures, FOMC minutes, and delayed economic reports. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.79%, 0.38%, and 0.15%, respectively, at 8:36 a.m. EST on February 17.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Last week, all three major indexes closed lower. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones each dropped more than 1%, while the Nasdaq Composite fell over 2%, marking its fifth straight week of losses. Ongoing concerns about AI disrupting software, real estate, and financial services weighed on investor sentiment.

Turning to trending tickers, shares of health tech giant Masimo (MASI) rallied more than 34% in pre-market trading following reports that the company was nearing a deal with Danaher (DHR) to be acquired for about $10 billion in cash. Danaher stock declined about 6% on the news. Meanwhile, Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) stock jumped 6% following a Wall Street Journal report that Elliott Investment Management has built a more than 10% stake in the company.

Also, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) stock rose about 3% after Netflix (NFLX) granted Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) a seven-day waiver to hold deal talks with Paramount. WBD stock gained 2% on the news.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: PANW Earnings: Palo Alto Networks Stock Sinks Despite Top and Bottom Line Beats
data.description:
The stock of Palo Alto Networks (PANW) is down 5% despite the cybersecurity firm posting financial results that beat Wall Street forecasts on the top and bottom lines.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Santa Clara, California-based company reported fiscal second-quarter earnings per share (EPS) of $1.03, which topped the $0.94 consensus expectation of analysts. Revenue for the three months ended Jan. 31 totaled $2.59 billion, which narrowly beat the $2.58 billion forecast on Wall Street.

In terms of guidance, Palo Alto Networks said that it expects EPS of $0.78 to $0.80 on revenue of $2.94 billion to $2.95 billion. That outlook fell short of the $0.92 in earnings per share and sales of $2.60 billion that analysts had penciled in for the company.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Melius Research Turns Bullish on Chevron (CVX) Stock. Here's Why
data.description:
Melius Research upgraded Chevron (CVX) stock to Buy from Hold with a price target of $205, saying "Chevron's strategy couldn't be clearer: value over volume." The analyst turned bullish on the oil and gas giant, citing momentum in its exploration activity and the underappreciated upside presented by the opportunity in Venezuela.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Melius Analyst Upgrades CVX Stock to Buy

Melius analyst James West expects Chevron to benefit from increased exploration spending and new talent from the Hess acquisition. The analyst highlighted that the company has increased its acreage position by more than 50%, with the addition of ten new basin positions over the past two years. West also noted new opportunities in Libya and Iraq.

He added that Chevron anticipates drilling at least 10 to 15 exploration wells, with the Gulf of America, Libya, Namibia, Nigeria, Angola, Suriname, Brazil, and Guyana presenting attractive areas for exploration. West also highlighted that Chevron is in the best position to capture upside potential in Venezuela.

The analyst is also impressed by the leadership of CEO Mike Wirth, who he believes "ushered in an era of disciplined M&A, cash generation, and strategic growth."

Overall, West is optimistic about Chevron based on its differentiated investment profile that is highlighted by:

  • a focus on shareholder returns via dividends and buybacks
  • an increase in exploration activity focused on high-impact frontier areas
  • a shift to cash harvesting in the Permian Basin rather than growth
  • upside potential in Venezuela.

Is CVX Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Chevron stock based on 15 Buys and six Holds. The average CVX stock price target of $186.50 indicates 3.1% upside potential from current levels. Chevron stock has risen about 19% so far in 2026. CVX stock offers a dividend yield of 4.5%.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: 3 Best Value Stocks with Over 15% Upside in 2026, According to Analysts
data.description:
Investors looking for undervalued stocks in the current volatile market may want to pay close attention to Wall Street's latest picks. These stocks stand out for their solid fundamentals, attractive valuations, and catalysts that could drive meaningful gains in the months ahead.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Before we go further, let's define value stocks. These are companies that look undervalued compared to their true worth, trading at lower price-to-earnings or price-to-book ratios. When the market eventually recognizes their real value, they can deliver strong gains.

Now, let's check the three value stocks that analysts have spotlighted. Each offers more than 15% upside potential from current levels.

Here Are This Week's Stocks

IBM (IBM) – This global technology company focuses on hybrid cloud, AI, and enterprise consulting services. It has a Moderate Buy analyst consensus rating and an average price target of $337.53, implying a 30.71% upside potential from the current levels. The company's P/E of 23.5x reflects a 21.5% discount to the Technology sector's median of 29.92.

Recently, short seller Jim Chanos said IBM faces more AI risk than reward, arguing the company now leans on consulting, India‑centric services, and acquisitions even as its stock trades at a premium to larger tech peers.

Cisco Systems (CSCO) – This technology company designs networking equipment, cybersecurity solutions, and enterprise software powering the internet and corporate infrastructure. Its average price target of $91.82 implies a 19.36% upside potential from the current levels. CSCO stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating. Trading at a P/E of 27x, the company is valued 9.7% below the Technology sector's median multiple of 29.92.

Last week, Cisco reported revenue of about $15.35 billion and adjusted EPS of $1.04, both above expectations. However, the stock fell as rising memory costs pressured margins and tempered guidance.

Sprouts Farmers (SFM) – This American grocery retailer is focused on fresh, natural, organic, and better-for-you foods, blending a farmers-market feel with everyday grocery offerings. It has a Strong Buy analyst consensus rating and an average price target of $104.71, implying a 59.02% upside potential from the current levels. With a P/E ratio of 13.39x, the stock is priced at a 44.2% discount to the Consumer Defensive sector's median of 24.

The company is scheduled to report Q4 earnings on February 19. Wall Street analysts expect Sprouts Farmers to report earnings of $0.89 per share on revenue of $2.15 billion.

data.title: Robinhood to Launch Private Markets Fund for Retail Investors – These Are Its Holdings
data.description:
Trading platform Robinhood (HOOD) has introduced Robinhood Ventures Fund I, which will be the first fund under its new Robinhood Ventures platform. The closed-end fund is expected to begin trading on the New York Stock Exchange in the coming weeks under the ticker RVI after completing its IPO, and Robinhood customers can request IPO shares at an expected price of $25 per share.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, RVI is built to provide exposure to a focused group of private companies. Its initial portfolio includes:

  • Airwallex
  • Boom
  • Databricks
  • Mercor
  • Oura
  • Ramp
  • Revolut

More companies are expected to be added over time. In addition, the fund has agreed to purchase shares of Stripe, with the deal expected to close after the IPO. This launch comes as fewer companies go public, while those that do eventually go public remain private for longer.

In fact, according to data from Apollo Academy and the Federal Reserve, there are now 6.5 times more private companies than public ones, and U.S. private companies are valued at more than $10 trillion combined. With that in mind, Robinhood says that the fund supports its mission of making investing more accessible by adding daily liquidity as a publicly traded fund. Moreover, the fund will charge a 2% annual management fee on net assets, calculated quarterly, although it will drop to 1% for the first six months after the IPO.

Is HOOD a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on HOOD stock based on 14 Buys, two Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average HOOD price target of $130 per share implies 73% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Game On, Again: Warner Bros. Discovery Stock (NASDAQ:WBD) Opens Door to Paramount Skydance
data.description:
The ongoing saga of entertainment giant Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) just developed a new wrinkle today. Earlier, we heard that Warner may reopen the bidding process to let Paramount Skydance (PSKY) make another play. Now, the possibility is reality, and Paramount is back in to try and get Warner out from under Netflix (NFLX). Shareholders were ecstatic, and sent Warner shares surging up over 3% in the closing minutes of Tuesday's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Netflix reportedly gave Warner seven days to explore a new offer, and indeed, Paramount made that offer. Paramount reportedly plans to raise its bid from $30 per share to $31, and there may be further enhancement to come. This represents a significant difference between Paramount's offer and Netflix's somehow winning bid of $27.75 per share. Paramount also pointed out that its bid was likely to get the nod from regulators.

Currently, the Warner board is still advising shareholders to ignore the higher price and better regulatory welcome from Paramount's deal and instead take Netflix's bid. Paramount wants the entirety of Warner, including the legacy cable stations, while Netflix is only after the studios and streaming portion.

The Vote is On

The final vote for shareholders, meanwhile, is still set to occur on March 20. Warner's board is still maintaining its position that Netflix's deal is superior, despite the fact that it does not take the entirety of Warner into account, requires a cable spin-off to take place, and is actually lower than Paramount's bid.

The major objections to Paramount's bid, reports note, are the need for "significant debt financing" to make it happen, as well as the need for "…absolute clarity as to funding obligations and certainty of funding at closing, or to pay damages if due." Paramount has not only raised its per-share bid, reports note, but also added a "ticking fee," as well as the $2.8 billion that Netflix would receive should Warner call off the deal.

Is WBD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on WBD stock based on four Buys and seven Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 163.56% rally in its share price over the past year, the average WBD price target of $29.13 per share implies 1.32% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
